Title: Inside BYD: CT Scans of a Battery Cell, Switch Panel, Charger, and Key Fob from the EV Banned in America
Description: BYD sold 4.6M vehicles in 2025, but Americans can't buy one. We CT scanned four components from its lineup to reveal what vertical integration looks like at the part level.

Title: High-agency strategy
Description: Matt Ström-Awn · Designer, leader, and coach focused on building exceptional products and teams.

Title: Claude Code Isn't About Code
Description: The people getting the most out of Claude Code aren't writing code. Some notes on where the value moved, picking up where the disposable tools manifesto left off.

Title: The Abundance Mirage: why the road to hell is paved in unchallenged assumptions
Description: In the whole wasteland of AI discourse, there is perhaps no more dangerous proposition than "Abundance is universally good", a statement that is almost too tautological to be challenged. To illustrate my point, I'm going to walk you through a thought exercise in designing a dictatorship from the ground up without employing any obvious mechanisms for oppression.

Title: Building Analytics for Haxball, a Game I’ve Played Since 2011
Description: I built a live and historical analytics dashboard for Haxball that turns match data into player stats, team stats, charts, summaries, and xG.

Title: The Permanent Underclass
Description:
There’s this phrase that’s taking root in our Silicon Valley discourse. It drives me nuts and I’m making a clarion call for us to push back against its toxic ideology.
It’s The Permanent Underclass.
I can’t say how widely the phrase has spread but in my corner of the world - Silicon Valley, Technology - it comes up often enough that I assume most of my peers have at least heard it even if they are not regularly discussing it.

Title: Good Agents
Description: The spirit of Radical Speed Month includes an impetus to experiment. So experiment I have.

Title: Should CEOs Fire Their Software Developers? | Spicer Matthews

Title: Web3 means Freedom Software
Description: Web3's promise of user sovereignty remains unfulfilled—corrupted by venture-driven growth pressures that have strayed far from its original vision. True web3 must embody censorship resistance, open source freedom, privacy, and security, principles

At work, we made a decision to go with a table per tenant approach and

Title: Inherited the frontend of a 150K-user app, mostly vibe coded - Kool Codez
Description: The product runs the whole business and has 150K users. The codebase underneath needs a lot of work. Where do you draw the line on quality when this many people depend on it?

Title: The Internet Needs More Cross-Pollinators
Description: So, my silly little fanfic project blew up like crazy and received some really negative feedback. And I think I understand why. Exploring the concept of boundary spanning and cross-pollination in online communities. Drawing on organizational theory and the work of Michael Tushman. We need people who move between different online subcultures to seed ideas and build bridges.

Title: Arcan: 10 Years of Online Obscurity

Description: By this I mean, do you think your entire job could be done by an AI. Can your entire team / field / department be removed without issue?

Description: Slopfluencer: posts on model training, evaluations, agents, NLP, and building products with generative AI.

Title: Anthropic and the caravel problem · Valentin Radu
Description: In 1500 a country of a million people invented the ship that opened the world, got rich beyond its size, and then lost everything to latecomers with deeper pockets. Frontier AI labs are sailing the same caravel. Why the model was never the moat, and the two narrow ways out.
